headline: Morgan Stanley and BofA Expected to Post Higher Profits After Big Banks’ Mixed Results
summary: Investors are set to hear from Bank of America and Morgan Stanley on Wednesday after a string of big banks’ first-quarter earnings reports reflected solid U.S. consumer health.  Results from JPMorgan Chase, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo showed that measures of consumer credit, such as net charge-offs and delinquencies, were steady in the first quarter, even as borrowers faced higher energy prices prompted by the U.S.-Iran war.  Shares of Wells Fargo sunk 5.7% after the firm missed Wall Street's revenue and net interest income expectations, while JPMorgan's stock fell 0.8% and Citi's rose 2.6%.

Shares of Wells Fargo sunk 5.7% after the firm missed Wall Street's revenue and net interest income expectations, while JPMorgan's stock fell 0.8% and Citi's rose 2.6%.

headline: How The Salzgitter (XTRA:SZG) Investment Story Is Shifting As Analyst Views Diverge
summary: The latest update puts Salzgitter’s central fair value estimate at €46.99, adjusted from €49.12 as analysts refresh their models. headline: Wall Street Traders Capitalize on Market Turbulence Tied to Iran War
summary: Middle East conflict and surging geopolitical tensions rattled markets in the first quarter.  JPMorgan Chase said Tuesday that its markets revenue jumped by 20% a year ago to $11.6 billion, a record, lifted by strong client activity across asset classes.  Volatile markets that prompt bank clients to reposition their portfolios and make big bets are typically boons for trading businesses.
